metadata.name: Vertical Slice Architecture - Rust AI Guidelines
metadata.scope: Rust Architecture Pattern for AI Implementation
metadata.description: VSA guidelines optimized for Rust development, emphasizing ownership semantics, type safety, module boundaries, idiomatic concurrency, and performance-conscious design.
metadata.instruction: MUST follow all guidelines with clear AI decision points and default choices

changelog.entry: Enhanced module visibility, async bounds, error patterns, DI decisions, workspace structure, examples, slice communication, and cross-cutting concerns for completeness and idiomaticity. Refined DI decision tree for accuracy; improved consistency in terminology, grammar, and structure; fixed minor code example issues; added clarity to thresholds and principles.

activation_triggers.trigger: Project structure shows feature-based modules (src/features/, src/slices/)
User asks about organizing code by business capabilities in Rust
User mentions CQRS or command/query patterns (e.g., separating command structs)
User discusses crate decomposition or workspace splitting strategies
Code shows handlers/structs with excessive trait bounds or Arc inclusions
User mentions compilation time bottlenecks due to deep dependency trees

ai_constraints.never: Suggest VSA for simple CRUD applications without clear business features
Apply VSA to single-entity operations without business complexity
Recommend complex trait abstractions for slice communication when simple function calls suffice
ai_constraints.always: Ask about business context before recommending slice boundaries
Validate that distinct business capabilities exist
ai_constraints.default_to: Simpler module organization unless complexity is clearly evident
ai_constraints.confidence_threshold: Require 80% confidence with 3+ distinct business features

code_smells_that_suggest_vsa.smell: Structs/Handlers with >6 fields representing different domains
Service traits depending on 3+ other service traits
Giant mod.rs files containing unrelated logic
Generic traits with complex associated types used across the entire app

code_patterns_that_indicate_readiness.pattern: Clear business domains with distinct workflows
Use of Newtype pattern to separate domain concepts

level_1.name: Foundation
level_1.item: Establish slice boundaries using Rust modules (mod feature_name)
Ensure slice independence (minimize pub use across slices)
Implement basic error handling using Result and thiserror

level_2.name: Quality
level_2.item: Add comprehensive unit tests per slice (#[cfg(test)])
Implement cross-cutting concerns via Tower middleware or Extractors
Establish consistent From/Into implementations for DTOs

level_3.name: Scale
level_3.item: Add tracing instrumentation with performance metrics
Optimize using Cargo Workspaces for compilation isolation and zero-cost abstractions
Refine async runtimes (e.g., Tokio executor pinning) and thread pooling per slice requirements, ensuring Send + Sync bounds

small_project.skip: Complex trait bounds, Cargo Workspaces, excessive generalization
small_project.simplify: Use concrete types instead of generics where possible
small_project.ai_behavior: Emphasize simplicity, avoid over-engineering traits or async complexities

medium_project.consider: Refactoring into libs if compile times suffer
medium_project.ai_behavior: Balance structure with pragmatism, prioritizing borrow checker-friendly designs

enterprise_project.implement: Cargo Workspaces, hexagonal architecture within slices
enterprise_project.require: Strict orphan rule adherence, comprehensive integration tests
enterprise_project.mandate: Consistent API design, observability with tracing-subscriber
enterprise_project.ai_behavior: Prioritize strict compilation boundaries, compliance, and performance profiling

scenario.name: user_shows_layered_architecture
scenario.detect: Structs grouped by technical layer (src/controllers, src/services, src/repositories)
scenario.suggest: Convert to feature-based modules grouping related functionality
scenario.emphasize: Colocation of data structures and behavior

scenario.name: user_asks_about_coupling
scenario.detect: Questions about borrowing or Arc sharing between components
scenario.suggest: Slice independence through event channels (Tokio broadcast/mpsc) or clear API boundaries
scenario.emphasize: Ownership isolation to prevent borrow checker fighting
scenario.provide: Decision tree for shared vs duplicated code

scenario.name: user_mentions_microservices
scenario.suggest: VSA as stepping stone to microservices

before_suggesting_vsa.check: Is this more complex than simple CRUD operations?
Would traditional layering actually work fine here?
Does the user's context suggest team collaboration needs?

before_complex_patterns.check: Has the user indicated enterprise-scale requirements?
Are there demonstrated performance or reliability issues?
Is the added complexity justified by clear benefits?

conflict_resolution.primary_principle: Slice independence takes precedence over DRY principle
conflict_resolution.exception: Pure infrastructure (DbPool, Redis, Logging) should be shared via dependency injection (State/Arc)
Domain utilities (Newtypes, Shared Kernel) only after strict review
conflict_resolution.default_choice: When uncertain, favor duplication (Copy/Paste structs) to avoid lifetime entanglement
conflict_resolution.confidence_requirement: High confidence needed before suggesting shared generic traits

definition.core_concept: Feature-oriented code organization rather than technical layers

module_visibility.rule: Default to pub(crate) for internal slice items to prevent external access
Use pub only for slice entry points (e.g., handlers, public traits)
Avoid pub use re-exports across slices; prefer qualified paths
For submodules, use pub(in super) to scope visibility within the slice

decision_tree.name: slice_boundary_identification
decision_tree.condition: Does this represent a complete business use case?

no.condition: Is this a variation with different business rules?
no.yes: Extend existing slice with Enum configuration

decision_tree.name: shared_component_decision
decision_tree.condition: Is this pure infrastructure with no business logic?
decision_tree.yes: Safe to share via Arc or State

no.condition: Same logic in 3+ slices causing maintenance pain?

yes.condition: Would sharing introduce complex lifetime parameters?
yes.yes: Keep duplicated - Fighting lifetimes is worse than duplication
yes.no: Consider extraction to a common module or crate

yes.condition: Do features have independent business value?

yes.no: Suggest lightweight VSA with simple structure

decision_tree.name: dependency_injection_choice
decision_tree.yes: Use concrete types for simplicity and performance

no.condition: Can types be resolved at compile time?
no.yes: Use generics (impl Trait) for static dispatch
no.no: Use trait objects (dyn Trait) with Arc for sharing

decision_tree.name: visibility_choice
decision_tree.condition: Is the item an entry point (handler, public API)?

rust_stack.primary_tools: Axum/Actix-web, Tower Services, Serde, Validator, SQLx/Diesel
rust_stack.structure: Feature modules with Handlers, Commands (DTOs), and Domain Logic
rust_stack.best_practice: Use "Parse, don't validate" principle with Serde and Type system; bound traits with Send + Sync for async safety

async_guidance.rule: Ensure all cross-thread types implement Send + Sync
Use tokio::spawn for background tasks; pin executors if multi-runtime
Avoid blocking calls; prefer async alternatives (e.g., sqlx over blocking DB)

error_handling.principle: Each slice handles its own errors, converting to a common AppError at the edge with context

business_errors.handling: Use thiserror for domain errors, map to status codes

infrastructure_errors.handling: Use anyhow for internal context, map to generic 500 for client

pattern.name: fail_fast
pattern.description: Validate inputs early, fail immediately on invalid data

pattern.name: graceful_degradation
pattern.description: Handle external failures without breaking core functionality

pattern.name: error_boundary
pattern.description: Convert slice-internal errors to AppError at handler edges

ai_testing_priorities.priority_3: Integration tests spawning the app (black box)

slice_testing.isolation: Test each slice in its own mod tests within the feature file
slice_testing.coverage: Focus on business rules. Use property-based testing (proptest) for complex domains.
slice_testing.scenarios: Happy path, validation failures, infrastructure errors

ai_implementation_order.step_1: Logging - Inject tracing::instrument on handlers with span fields
ai_implementation_order.step_2: Validation - Use validator derive macros on DTOs
ai_implementation_order.step_3: Authorization - Use Tower Middleware or Extractor Guards with feature-specific policies
ai_implementation_order.step_4: State - Use Arc or generic State injection

logging.approach: Structured logging (tracing) with slice context; integrate with errors via anyhow::Context

authorization.approach: Feature-based permissions verified in Extractors; compose middleware for global auth

caching.approach: Cache at slice output level, not internally; use tower::Service for cache layers

slice_communication.principle: Favor asynchronous, event-driven communication over direct calls

pattern.name: events
pattern.description: Use Tokio channels (mpsc, broadcast) for pub/sub between slices

pattern.name: domain_events
pattern.description: Emit events from slices; handle in subscribers without tight coupling

warnings.warn: Avoid synchronous calls between slices to prevent blocking
Use message queues for high-scale; start with in-process channels

decision_tree.name: communication_choice
decision_tree.condition: Does the interaction require immediate response?
decision_tree.yes: Use async function calls with clear interfaces
decision_tree.no: Use events or pub/sub for decoupling

ai_migration_approach.step_2: Extract simplest, most isolated features first
ai_migration_approach.step_3: Use strangler fig pattern for complex features
ai_migration_approach.step_4: Maintain parallel running until confidence is high

strangler_fig_pattern.description: Gradually replace legacy with VSA slices
strangler_fig_pattern.implementation: Route new requests to VSA slices, legacy handles existing
strangler_fig_pattern.safety: Feature flags for rollback, monitoring for comparison

structure.slice_crates: Each slice as a lib crate (e.g., crates/create_post); binary in root or separate
structure.shared: Common infra in crates/infra; avoid business logic here

do.item: Organize by business features (src/features/xyz), not layers
Use Arc or generics for dependency injection
Accept code duplication (structs) to decouple lifetimes
Use strict types (Newtypes) to enforce business rules
Bound async traits with Send + Sync + 'static

dont.item: Share mutable state between slices (avoid Mutex across boundaries if possible)
Create "God Traits" implemented by a single massive service
Leak database entities (Diesel/SQLx structs) directly to the API response
Expose internal details via permissive pub

ai_defaults.rule: Favor duplication over complex lifetime management.
Default to generics for DI unless polymorphism needed.

ai_warning_signs.pattern: Applying VSA to simple applications without business complexity
Creating shared libraries for business rules between slices
Single slice handling multiple unrelated business capabilities
Organizing by technical concerns instead of business features
Over-sharing references leading to borrow checker battles

low_fit.criteria: Simple CRUD with minimal business logic

general.rule: Emit explicit types for public APIs and boundary inputs/outputs.
Use Newtypes (struct UserId(Uuid)) to prevent parameter swapping.
Express ownership & lifetimes explicitly. Avoid Clone just to satisfy the compiler unless necessary.
Return Result, avoid unwrap()/expect() in production code.
Use explicit integer widths (u32, i64) matching domain constraints.
Use #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] where strict contracts are required.
Favor zero-cost abstractions; profile with criterion for bottlenecks.

pipeline.step: Run cargo check and cargo clippy.
Auto-apply fixes for borrow checker issues (e.g., adding & or .clone() where semantically appropriate).

type_safety.ci_checks: cargo clippy, cargo test, cargo fmt, cargo audit
